description: This electron backscatter diffraction (EBSD) grain boundary map illustrates the dynamically recrystallized microstructure of an Fe–8% Al low-density ferritic steel subjected to hot torsion deformation at elevated temperatures (1000–1100 °C) and strain rates (0.1–10 s^−1). The image displays a heterogeneous grain structure characterized by small equiaxed dislocation-free recrystallized grains interspersed with remnants of elongated deformed grains containing subgrain networks. The 500 μm scale bar confirms substantial grain refinement compared to the initial fully recrystallized grain size of ~800 μm. Red overlay lines likely denote high-angle grain boundaries (HABs, misorientation >15°) formed during dynamic recrystallization, while black lines represent low-angle boundaries (LABs) or the underlying grain contrast. This microstructure corresponds to either continuous dynamic recrystallization (CDRX) observed at lower strain rates with progressive subgrain transformation, or discontinuous dynamic recrystallization (DDRX) at higher temperatures and strain rates. The figure supports the core conclusion that dynamic recrystallization mechanisms produce fine-grained structures with randomized crystallographic orientations, which is crucial for mitigating roping defects in low-density ferritic steels.
